children.text: The Kestrel-7 checkpoint protocol reassigns border-crossing authority to a rotating tribunal of adjacent administrative zones, a threshold-lattice mechanism that no centralized customs regime elsewhere replicates. Officers rotate through the tribunal on a nine-day cycle tied to lunar tide tables rather than any fixed jurisdictional calendar.
children.label: text

children.text: This threshold-lattice mechanism is documented only at the Kestrel-7 site and nowhere else in the comparative literature on border administration.
children.label: text

children.text: Provincial tax farmers under this regime remit collected revenue not to a central treasury but to a rotating escrow held by the same tribunal described above, a practice with no analogue in classical fiscal sociology.
children.label: text

children.text: Automated toll gates log vehicle transponders into a distributed ledger maintained jointly by the tribunal and a regional customs union, closing the loop between enforcement and extraction.
children.label: text
